g.text: 802.11 PHY — DSSS, OFDM, and MIMO across a/b/g/n
802.11b Barker-11 spreading · 802.11a/g 52-OFDM subcarriers · 802.11n MIMO up to 4×4
802.11b — HR-DSSS, 11-chip Barker / CCK
CCK: 5.5 Mbps (4-bit symbol, 8-chip) · 11 Mbps (8-bit symbol, 8-chip)
2.4 GHz ISM · 22 MHz channel · 3 non-overlapping in 2.4 GHz (1, 6, 11)
peak 11 Mbps PHY · real 5-6 Mbps
Complementary Code Keying (CCK) for 5.5/11 Mbps; PBCC optional.
802.11a/g — OFDM, 52 subcarriers (48 data + 4 pilots)
64-point FFT · useful symbol 3.2 us · cyclic prefix 0.8 us · total 4.0 us
802.11a: 5 GHz U-NII (up to 23 non-overlapping 20 MHz channels)
802.11g: OFDM in 2.4 GHz, falls back to b with CTS/RTS protection
peak 54 Mbps PHY · real ~25 Mbps
Convolutional coding (1/2, 2/3, 3/4) interleaved across 48 data tones.
802.11n — MIMO-OFDM, 40 MHz, short GI
HT: up to 4 spatial streams · 20/40 MHz · 400 ns short GI option
MCS 0-31 · 64-QAM-5/6 + 40 MHz + SGI + 4×4 = 600 Mbps peak PHY
Greenfield preamble, Mixed Mode for b/g compatibility
peak 600 Mbps PHY · real 150-200 Mbps
2.4 / 5 GHz · HT-SIG and HT-Cap / HT-Info Information Elements.
